benefits.benefit_desc: Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, reduces stress levels by acting as an adaptogen, which helps the body resist the physical and chemical effects of stress. The main active constituents that contribute to this benefit are oleanolic acid, ursolic acid, rosmarinic acid, eugenol, and cirsilineol. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that provide this stress-relieving benefit are the leaves and the seeds, which contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The most common medicinal preparations of Holy Basil used to reduce stress include dried leaves for tea, powdered leaves in capsules, and tinctures or essential oils extracted from the leaves. By reducing stress levels, Holy Basil can help alleviate symptoms of related health conditions such as anxiety, depression, insomnia, and adrenal fatigue.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, fights inflammation, meaning it can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the body caused by inflammatory conditions. The main active constituents in Holy Basil that give it this benefit are oleanolic acid, ursolic acid, and eugenol. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that have anti-inflammatory properties are the leaves and seeds. The main medicinal preparations of Holy Basil that are used to fight inflammation include dried leaves, powder, and essential oil. The main health conditions that this benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum helps relieve include arthritis, muscle sprains, and other inflammatory conditions.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, lowers fevers, meaning it helps to reduce body temperature during feverish states. The main active constituents in Ocimum tenuiflorum that are responsible for its fever-reducing properties are thought to be the essential oil components, such as eugenol, methyl eugenol, and Ursolic acid. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that have this fever-reducing effect are the leaves and seeds. The main medicinal preparations of Ocimum tenuiflorum that are used to lower fevers include teas, tinctures, and essential oil applications. This benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum is particularly helpful in relieving fevers associated with common illnesses such as colds, flu, and other viral or bacterial infections.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, reduces pain, meaning it can help alleviate discomfort and soreness in the body. The main active constituents that give Holy Basil this pain-relieving property are thought to be its essential oil, which contains eugenol, and its terpenes, such as ursolic and oleanolic acids. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that are used for this benefit are the leaves, which are often made into a tea or powdered for use in supplements. The main medicinal preparations of Holy Basil that are used to reduce pain include dried leaves, essential oil, and powdered extracts. This benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum is particularly helpful for individuals experiencing headaches, muscle and joint pain, and general inflammation.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, protects against infection, meaning it helps to prevent the spread of harmful microorganisms within the body. The main active constituents in Ocimum tenuiflorum that provide this benefit are Ursolic acid, Eugenol, and Rosmarinic acid. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that possess this infection-fighting property are the leaves and seeds. The main medicinal preparations of Ocimum tenuiflorum that provide this benefit include dried leaves, essential oil, and powdered extract. This benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum is particularly helpful in relieving conditions such as respiratory infections, urinary tract infections, and skin infections.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, improves respiratory health by helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory ailments such as asthma, bronchitis, and nasal congestion. The main active constituents of Ocimum tenuiflorum that contribute to this benefit are eugenol, ursolic acid, and carvacrol. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that provide this benefit are the leaves and seeds, which contain high concentrations of these beneficial compounds. Medicinal preparations of Ocimum tenuiflorum that provide this benefit include teas, essential oils, and capsules. The main health conditions that this benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum helps relieve include inflammation of the respiratory tract, difficulty breathing, and excessive mucus production.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, lowers blood sugar levels, meaning it can help regulate glucose metabolism and maintain healthy blood sugar levels. The main active constituents of Ocimum tenuiflorum that contribute to this benefit are oleanolic acid, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that provide this benefit are the leaves and seeds. The main medicinal preparations of Ocimum tenuiflorum that give this benefit include dried leaves for tea, powdered leaf extract, and essential oil. This benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum helps relieve health conditions such as type 2 diabetes, metabolic syndrome, and insulin resistance.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, reduces cholesterol levels by containing bioactive compounds that inhibit cholesterol synthesis and increase excretion. The main active constituents responsible for this benefit are Ursolic acid, Oleanolic acid, and Eugenol, which are found in the leaves and seeds of the plant. Medicinal preparations such as dried leaves, powder, and extracts are commonly used to reduce cholesterol levels. By reducing cholesterol, Holy Basil helps relieve conditions such as hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis, and other cardiovascular diseases.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, improves digestion by helping to break down food and promoting the healthy functioning of the digestive system. The main active constituents in Holy Basil that contribute to this benefit are its essential oils, which include eugenol, carvacrol, and ursolic acid. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that provide this benefit are the leaves, which are rich in these essential oils. The main medicinal preparations of Holy Basil that give this benefit include teas, tinctures, and capsules made from the leaves of the plant. This benefit of improved digestion from Holy Basil helps relieve a variety of health conditions, including gastric irritation, dyspepsia, and symptoms of Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) such as bloating and discomfort.

Holy Basil, also known as Ocimum tenuiflorum, protects the liver by potentially reducing liver enzyme levels, thereby decreasing the stress on the liver and promoting its healthy function. The main active constituents in Holy Basil that contribute to this benefit are thought to be its essential oil, which includes eugenol, methyl eugenol, and methyl chavicol, as well as its phenolic compounds, such as rosmarinic and caffeic acids. The primary parts of the Ocimum tenuiflorum plant that provide this benefit are the leaves and the seeds, which are commonly used in various medicinal preparations. These preparations can include dried or fresh leaves used in teas, capsules or tablets containing dried leaf powder, and alcohol-based extracts. The main health conditions that this benefit of Ocimum tenuiflorum helps relieve include liver damage caused by toxins, stress, or infection, as well as potentially supporting liver function in those with liver disease.
